name: Solomon Islands

visa_free.nationality: All nationalities not on the prior-approval list (visitor permit granted on arrival)
visa_free.notes: Visitors with a valid passport (6 months validity), return/onward ticket, and sufficient funds receive a visitor's permit on arrival for up to 3 months in any 12-month period (Immigration Act). The specific Tourist Visa product on the immigration.gov.sb/visitor-visa-tourist/ page describes an initial 30-day on-arrival grant with extension to 150 days available. Countries requiring PRIOR APPROVAL from the Director of Immigration before arrival are listed in visa_policy.notes.

visa_free.nationality: MSG citizens (Fiji, Papua New Guinea, Vanuatu) - Melanesian Spearhead Group visa
visa_free.notes: Citizens of MSG member states (Fiji, PNG, Vanuatu) receive a complimentary 60-day single-entry visitor visa with no extension, no documents required. Listed under 'MSG (Melanesian Spearhead Group)' visitor visa category at no charge.

visa_free.nationality: EU member states (all except Ireland; France and Netherlands: European territory only)
visa_free.notes: EU-Solomon Islands Short-Stay Visa Waiver Agreement (signed 7 October 2016, provisionally applied from 8 October 2016, formally concluded by Council Decision 2017/235 of 7 February 2017). Covers holders of valid ordinary, diplomatic, service, official, or special passports of all EU member states. UK excluded (not EU at time of agreement). Ireland excluded by agreement text. France and Netherlands: European territory only. Maximum 90 days in any 180-day period. Work/paid activities not permitted.

visa_free.nationality: China (ordinary passports) - Mutual Visa Exemption Agreement for ordinary passports
visa_free.notes: Mutual Visa Exemption Agreement for ordinary passport holders signed 7 November 2024; entered into force 28 December 2024. Chinese ordinary passport holders may enter Solomon Islands visa-free for up to 30 days per single visit, with a cumulative maximum of 90 days within any 180-day period. Hong Kong and Macau SARs excluded. Work, study, settlement, and journalism require a visa. Per immigration.gov.sb/visas/, China is also listed under the Mutual Reciprocal Visa pre-approval pathway (90 days) as an alternative channel.

visa_policy.notes: Solomon Islands issues visitor permits on arrival to nationals of most countries who hold a valid passport (6+ months validity), return ticket, and sufficient funds. Nationals of the following countries require PRIOR APPROVAL from the Director of Immigration before arrival: Africa - Algeria, Angola, Benin, Botswana, Burkina Faso, Cameroon, Cape Verde, Central African Republic, Chad, Congo, Democratic Republic of Congo, Comoros, Côte d'Ivoire, Djibouti, Egypt, Equatorial Guinea, Eritrea, Ethiopia, Gabon, Gambia, Ghana, Guinea, Guinea-Bissau, Kenya, Lesotho, Liberia, Libya, Madagascar, Malawi, Mali, Mauritania, Mauritius, Mozambique, Morocco, Namibia, Niger, Nigeria, Rwanda, São Tomé & Príncipe, Senegal, Seychelles, Sierra Leone, Somalia, South Africa, Sudan, Swaziland, Tanzania, Togo, Tunisia, Uganda, Zimbabwe; Asia - Afghanistan, Bahrain, Bangladesh, Bhutan, Myanmar, Cambodia, China (note: Chinese ordinary passport holders are now entitled to visa-free access under the December 2024 agreement - the prior-approval listing on the UN Mission page may not yet reflect this update), Hong Kong, India, Indonesia, Iran, Iraq, Jordan, Kazakhstan, North Korea, Laos, Lebanon, Mongolia, Nepal, Oman, Pakistan, Philippines, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, Sri Lanka, Syria, Tajikistan, Tibet, Turkey, Turkmenistan, Vietnam, Yemen, UAE, Uzbekistan; Europe - Albania, Azerbaijan, Bosnia Herzegovina, Bulgaria, Croatia, Cyprus, Armenia, Estonia, Georgia, Kosovo, Latvia, Lithuania, Moldova, Romania, Russia, Ukraine, North Macedonia; Caribbean - Cuba, Haiti, Jamaica; Oceania - Kiribati, Tuvalu; Central America - Costa Rica, El Salvador, Guatemala, Honduras, Mexico, Nicaragua, Panama; South America - Bolivia, Colombia, Ecuador, Venezuela. Note: China, India, UAE, and Indonesia nationals who have applied for and received a Mutual Reciprocal Visa grant letter before arrival may enter under that arrangement (90 days, see conditional_access). Chinese ordinary passport holders are also entitled to visa-free on-arrival access under the December 28, 2024 agreement (30 days/visit, 90 days/180-day period). No e-visa or ETA system is currently operational (site notes 'ONLINE PAYMENT COMING SOON' only).

conditional_access.conditions: Mutual Visa Exemption Agreement between People's Republic of China and Solomon Islands, in force 24 November 2022. Covers holders of valid diplomatic passports, service passports, and passports for public affairs (gongwu/official) of the PRC. Stay not to exceed 30 days from date of entry. Work prohibited. A separate ordinary passport agreement entered into force 28 December 2024 (see visa_policy.visa_free).

Mutual Reciprocal Visa arrangement between India and Solomon Islands, established 21 June 2022 per immigration.gov.sb. Covers Indian citizens holding official, diplomatic, or ordinary passports. Validity: 90 days, single entry, fee exempted, work prohibited. Applicants MUST apply before arrival via the eVisa portal and present the grant letter on entry - this is a pre-approval required arrangement, not a traditional visa-on-arrival or visa-free entry. India nationals otherwise require prior approval from Director of Immigration.

Mutual Reciprocal Visa arrangement between United Arab Emirates and Solomon Islands, officially in place as of September 2022 per immigration.gov.sb. Covers all passport classifications: ordinary, official, and diplomatic. Maximum 90 days, single entry, fee exempted, work prohibited. Applicants must apply before arrival via eVisa portal and present grant letter on entry. UAE nationals otherwise require prior approval from Director of Immigration.

Mutual Reciprocal Visa arrangement between Indonesia and Solomon Islands, extended to ordinary passports under subsequent arrangement per immigration.gov.sb. Covers diplomat, official, and ordinary passport holders. Maximum 90 days, single entry, fee exempted, work prohibited. Applicants must apply before arrival via eVisa portal and present grant letter on entry. Indonesian nationals otherwise require prior approval from Director of Immigration.

Mutual Visa Exemption Agreement between Kingdom of Saudi Arabia and Solomon Islands signed 9 October 2024 in Riyadh. Agreement covers holders of diplomatic, private (ordinary), and official passports ('short-stay visa requirements' mutually exempted per SPA press release title). Maximum stay duration not confirmed from any directly accessible official source (spa.gov.sa returned HTTP 403 during 2026-06-29 verification run; solomons.gov.sb page returned HTTP 307 redirect). Saudi nationals otherwise require prior approval from Director of Immigration.

visa_types.name: Visitor Visa - Tourist
visa_types.purpose: Allows a single entry for tourism, sightseeing, and leisure activities. Not for business or paid work.
visa_types.notes: On arrival grant is 30 days. Online application fee is $50 USD (discounted); in-person fee is $100 USD. Extendable up to 150 days total for $100 USD per extension. No work permitted. Online payment described as 'coming soon' - applications submitted in-person at Immigration Services Office in Honiara.

visa_types.name: Visitor Visa - Visiting Relatives
visa_types.purpose: Allows MSG passport holders to visit relatives resident in Solomon Islands for extended stays.
visa_types.notes: Eligibility restricted to MSG (Melanesian Spearhead Group) passport holders - citizens of Fiji, Papua New Guinea, and Vanuatu. Extendable up to 1 year.

visa_types.name: Visitor Visa - Yacht Person (Master & Crew)
visa_types.purpose: Covers masters and crew of private yachts entering Solomon Islands waters for tourism or transit purposes.
visa_types.notes: 60-day initial grant, non-extendable. Maximum aggregate stay of 180 days. Separate from the standard tourist visa.

visa_types.name: Visitor Visa - Journalist/Reporter
visa_types.purpose: Permits journalists and reporters to enter Solomon Islands for media and reporting activities.
visa_types.notes: Renewable. Requires media organisation details. 30-day initial grant.

visa_types.name: MSG (Melanesian Spearhead Group) Visitor Visa
visa_types.purpose: Complimentary visitor visa for citizens of MSG member states (Fiji, Papua New Guinea, Vanuatu) for tourism and visits.
visa_types.notes: Free of charge. No extension permitted. No supporting documents required beyond valid passport.

visa_types.name: Business Visa - Short-Term
visa_types.purpose: Permits foreign nationals to conduct short-term business activities such as meetings, negotiations, and conferences in Solomon Islands.
visa_types.notes: Not extendable. Single or multiple entry options listed. Work/employment not permitted - business activities only.

visa_types.name: Emergency Employment Visa
visa_types.purpose: Allows emergency short-term employment in Solomon Islands where a local worker is not immediately available.
visa_types.notes: Limited to 4 entries per year. Requires Labour Department support letter.

visa_types.name: Foreign Official/Diplomat Visa - Long-Term
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign officials and diplomats for long-term postings in Solomon Islands.
visa_types.notes: Fee exempted. Renewable. Work rights permitted within diplomatic capacity.

visa_types.name: Foreign Official/Diplomat Visa - Short-Term
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign officials and diplomats for short-term visits to Solomon Islands.
visa_types.notes: Fee exempted. No extension permitted. Requires third-party diplomatic note.

visa_types.name: Dependent of Diplomat Visa
visa_types.purpose: Issued to dependents (family members) of accredited foreign diplomats resident in Solomon Islands.
visa_types.notes: Fee exempted. Work rights not permitted.

visa_types.name: Ministerial Special Exemption Visa
visa_types.purpose: A special visa granted at the discretion of the Minister for exceptional cases not covered by standard visa categories.
visa_types.notes: Fee exempted. Renewable. Requires Ministerial support/approval.

visa_types.name: Dependent of Ministerial Special Exemption Visa
visa_types.purpose: Issued to dependents of holders of a Ministerial Special Exemption Visa.
visa_types.notes: Fee exempted. Renewable. Requires Ministerial support.

visa_types.name: Emergency Visa - 30 Days
visa_types.purpose: Issued for emergency short-stay situations requiring urgent entry into Solomon Islands.
visa_types.notes: Free of charge. Non-extendable. Requires Ministerial support.

visa_types.name: Emergency Visa - 3 Months
visa_types.purpose: Issued for emergency situations requiring a longer short-term stay in Solomon Islands.
visa_types.notes: Free of charge. Non-extendable. Requires Ministerial support.

visa_types.name: Aid Worker/Volunteer Visa (Non-Commercial)
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign aid workers and volunteers working for non-commercial organisations in Solomon Islands.
visa_types.notes: Extendable. Work rights permitted for volunteer/non-commercial activities only.

visa_types.name: Research Academic Visa
visa_types.purpose: Issued to researchers and academics conducting academic or scientific research in Solomon Islands.
visa_types.notes: Requires Ministerial support.

visa_types.name: Sportsperson Visa
visa_types.purpose: Issued to professional sportspersons competing or training in Solomon Islands.
visa_types.notes: Extendable. Requires Ministerial support.

visa_types.name: Religious Worker Visa (Non-Commercial)
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign religious workers and missionaries engaged in non-commercial religious activities in Solomon Islands.
visa_types.notes: Extendable. Requires Ministerial support.

visa_types.name: Mutual Reciprocal Visa - China
visa_types.purpose: Pre-approval visa arrangement allowing Chinese nationals to visit Solomon Islands under a mutual reciprocal agreement.
visa_types.notes: Fee exempted. 90 days accumulative, non-extendable. Work prohibited. Must apply via eVisa portal before arrival and present grant letter on entry. Note: Chinese ordinary passport holders are also entitled to visa-free on-arrival access (30 days/visit, 90 days/180-day period) under the December 28, 2024 agreement - this pathway is an alternative pre-approval route.

visa_types.name: Mutual Reciprocal Visa - EU Official Passport Holders
visa_types.purpose: Pre-approval reciprocal visa for EU official passport holders visiting Solomon Islands.
visa_types.notes: Fee exempted. Non-extendable. Work prohibited. Ordinary EU passport holders are visa-free under the 2016 EU-Solomon Islands agreement; this pathway specifically covers official passport holders applying via the mutual reciprocal arrangement.

visa_types.name: Mutual Reciprocal Visa - UAE
visa_types.purpose: Pre-approval visa arrangement for UAE nationals to visit Solomon Islands under a mutual reciprocal agreement.
visa_types.notes: Fee exempted. Non-extendable. Work prohibited. Must apply via eVisa portal before arrival and present grant letter on entry.

visa_types.name: Mutual Reciprocal Visa - India
visa_types.purpose: Pre-approval visa arrangement for Indian nationals to visit Solomon Islands under a mutual reciprocal agreement.
visa_types.notes: Fee exempted. Non-extendable. Work prohibited. Must apply via eVisa portal before arrival and present grant letter on entry.

visa_types.name: Mutual Reciprocal Visa - Indonesia
visa_types.purpose: Pre-approval visa arrangement for Indonesian nationals to visit Solomon Islands under a mutual reciprocal agreement.
visa_types.notes: Fee exempted. Non-extendable. Work prohibited. Must apply via eVisa portal before arrival and present grant letter on entry.

visa_types.name: Long-Term Employment Visa
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign nationals taking up long-term employment positions with registered businesses in Solomon Islands.
visa_types.notes: Extendable. Unlimited entries. Requires business name, type, and address details. Work rights permitted.

visa_types.name: Short-Term Employment Visa
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign nationals for short-term employment engagements in Solomon Islands.
visa_types.notes: Extendable. Requires Labour Department support letter.

visa_types.name: Dependent of Resident Visa (Short or Long-Term)
visa_types.purpose: Issued to dependents (spouses, children) of foreign residents holding a valid short or long-term employment visa.
visa_types.notes: Extendable. Linked to and co-expires with the primary applicant's visa.

visa_types.name: Family Relationship Visa - Solomon Islands Citizen Dependent
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign nationals who are dependents of Solomon Islands citizens, allowing long-term family residence.
visa_types.notes: Extendable. Work rights not automatically permitted - requires separate work authorisation.

visa_types.name: Permanent Resident Visa - Long-Term
visa_types.purpose: Grants permanent resident status to long-term foreign residents who have continuously resided in Solomon Islands.
visa_types.notes: Extendable (5-year grant). Requires minimum 5 years of continuous prior residence. Work rights not automatic - requires separate authorisation.

visa_types.name: Permanent Resident Visa - Dependent
visa_types.purpose: Issued to dependents of approved Permanent Resident Visa holders in Solomon Islands.
visa_types.notes: Free of charge. Linked to primary permanent resident holder. Re-evaluation required after 5 years.

visa_types.name: Film-maker Visa - Commercial
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign film-makers conducting commercial film production in Solomon Islands.
visa_types.notes: Extendable. Work rights permitted for project-specific commercial film production.

visa_types.name: Film-maker Visa - Non-Commercial
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign film-makers conducting non-commercial documentary or personal film projects in Solomon Islands.
visa_types.notes: Non-extendable. Work rights permitted within non-commercial scope only.

visa_types.name: Comedian Visa - Commercial
visa_types.purpose: Issued to professional comedians performing commercially in Solomon Islands.
visa_types.notes: Non-extendable. Requires Ministry/PM's Office support.

visa_types.name: Musician Visa - Commercial
visa_types.purpose: Issued to professional musicians performing commercially in Solomon Islands.
visa_types.notes: Non-extendable. Requires Ministry/PM's Office support.

visa_types.name: Church or Cultural Entertainer Visa (Non-Commercial)
visa_types.purpose: Issued to church groups and cultural entertainers for non-commercial performances and events in Solomon Islands.
visa_types.notes: Non-extendable. Requires Ministry/PM's Office support.

visa_types.name: Student Visa
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign nationals enrolled in accredited educational institutions in Solomon Islands.
visa_types.notes: Extendable. Requires confirmation letter from accredited institution. Dependents of students may be issued a separate student dependent visa under the same category at the same fee.

visa_types.name: Transit Visa - Commercial Maritime
visa_types.purpose: Issued to crew and passengers of commercial maritime vessels transiting Solomon Islands ports.
visa_types.notes: 7-day single entry granted on arrival. Maximum 14-day stay permitted. Extension possible only if a substantive visa application is lodged.

visa_types.name: Asylum Seeker Protection Visa
visa_types.purpose: Issued to persons who have lodged a formal asylum claim in Solomon Islands pending assessment of their refugee status.
visa_types.notes: Free of charge. Not extendable as a standalone grant - status depends on outcome of Ministerial assessment. Duration tied to assessment proceedings.

visa_types.name: Refugee Protection Visa
visa_types.purpose: Issued to persons formally recognised as refugees under international protection obligations.
visa_types.notes: Free of charge. Granted under specific international protection obligations. Not extendable.

visa_types.name: Human Trafficking and Smuggling Victim Visa
visa_types.purpose: Issued to identified victims of human trafficking and people smuggling to provide temporary protected status.
visa_types.notes: Free of charge. Protected status subject to formal assessment. Not extendable.

cbi.notes: As of June 2026, Solomon Islands does not have a functioning Citizenship by Investment program. A Citizenship by Investment Bill was drafted and included in the Government's 100-day agenda (announced September 2024 by PM Jeremiah Manele); it was undergoing peer review at the Attorney General's Chambers as of the last accessible official records. The bill has not been enacted into law and no official program with published investment amounts or application procedures has been launched. Third-party sources (Global Citizen Solutions, IMI Daily) referenced a possible 2025 launch but no official confirmation exists.

agreements: Melanesian Spearhead Group (MSG) - regional visa arrangement providing complimentary 60-day single-entry visitor visa for citizens of Fiji, Papua New Guinea, and Vanuatu

EU-Solomon Islands Short-Stay Visa Waiver Agreement (signed 7 October 2016, provisionally effective 8 October 2016, concluded by Council Decision 2017/235) - covers ALL passport types (ordinary, diplomatic, service, official, special), 90 days/180-day period, excludes Ireland and UK; France and Netherlands: European territory only

China-Solomon Islands Mutual Visa Exemption Agreement (diplomatic/service/official passports, in force 24 November 2022 - 30 days; ordinary passports agreement signed 7 November 2024, in force 28 December 2024 - 30 days per visit / 90 days per 180-day period; excludes Hong Kong and Macau SARs)
India-Solomon Islands Mutual Reciprocal Visa arrangement (established June 2022) - all passport types, 90 days, pre-approval application required before arrival
UAE-Solomon Islands Mutual Reciprocal Visa arrangement (September 2022) - all passport types, 90 days, pre-approval application required before arrival
Indonesia-Solomon Islands Mutual Reciprocal Visa arrangement (September 2022, extended to ordinary passports) - all passport types, 90 days, pre-approval application required before arrival
Saudi Arabia-Solomon Islands Mutual Visa Exemption Agreement (signed October 9, 2024) - diplomatic, private (ordinary), and official passports; exact stay duration unconfirmed from official source

validation_notes: 2026-06-29 RE-VERIFICATION: CONFIRMED immigration.gov.sb/visas/ lists mutual reciprocal visa arrangements for China, EU official passport holders, UAE, India, and Indonesia - Saudi Arabia NOT listed on this page as of this verification run. Page content verified.

2026-06-29 RE-VERIFICATION: CONFIRMED immigration.gov.sb/visitor-visa-tourist/ - on-arrival grant is 30 days for the specific Tourist Visa product. The general Immigration Act visitor permit allows up to 3 months (90 days) in any 12-month period. Both tracks exist; the 90-day figure in visa_free[0] reflects the Immigration Act maximum. Added clarifying note to visa_policy.visa_free[0] to distinguish the two tracks.

2026-06-29 RE-VERIFICATION: CONFIRMED immigration.gov.sb/mutual-visa-india/ - pre-approval required via eVisa portal, not visa-on-arrival. Covers ordinary, official, and diplomatic passports. 90 days single entry.
2026-06-29 RE-VERIFICATION: CONFIRMED immigration.gov.sb/mutual-visa-indonesia/ - pre-approval required via eVisa portal. Covers ordinary, official, diplomatic passports. 90 days.
2026-06-29 RE-VERIFICATION: CONFIRMED immigration.gov.sb/mutual-visa-uae/ - pre-approval required via eVisa portal. Covers ordinary, official, diplomatic passports. 90 days.

2026-06-29 RE-VERIFICATION: CONFIRMED UN Mission prior-approval list unchanged from prior run. China, India, UAE, Indonesia, and Saudi Arabia all remain on this list (some have bilateral arrangements that supersede the general prior-approval requirement).

2026-06-29 RE-VERIFICATION: Wikipedia lists Israel (90 days visa-free), Malaysia and Singapore (30 days visa-free) as having specific entitlements. Israel, Malaysia, and Singapore do NOT appear on the UN Mission prior-approval list, meaning they receive on-arrival visitor permits under the general rule. No specific bilateral agreement with Israel, Malaysia, or Singapore was found on any official Solomon Islands government source. No change made to visa_policy per safety rule (classify down, not up). Added to gaps.

2026-06-29 RE-VERIFICATION: Saudi Arabia - solomons.gov.sb/solomon-islands-and-saudi-arabia-sign-mutual-visa-exemption-agreement/ returned HTTP 307 redirect; spa.gov.sa/en/N2183858 returned HTTP 403. Agreement title confirmed covers 'diplomatic, private and official passports'. Source_official remains false; max_stay_days remains null. Saudi Arabia entry in conditional_access preserved as-is per safety rule.
2026-06-29 RE-VERIFICATION: NO CHANGE to CBI status. solomons.gov.sb pages continue to redirect. No CBI bill enactment confirmed.

PRIOR CORRECTIONS (from 2026-06-27 run): EU agreement source upgraded from solomonislandsembassy.com to EUR-Lex official treaty text; EU citizens correctly placed in visa_policy.visa_free; India/UAE/Indonesia pre-approval nature clarified; China ordinary passport agreement added to visa_policy.visa_free; China agreement entry-into-force date and stay terms confirmed.
gaps: The official immigration.gov.sb site does not publish a complete enumerated list of visa-free nationalities; the prior-approval country list (from the UN Mission page) was used to infer which nationalities receive on-arrival permits.

China ordinary passport agreement (in force 28 December 2024): the UN Mission prior-approval page still lists China - this page may be outdated and not yet reflect the December 2024 change. Confirmed agreement terms from Beijing government official source.

Saudi Arabia agreement: maximum stay duration not confirmed from any official source (spa.gov.sa returned HTTP 403; solomons.gov.sb page returned HTTP 307 redirect during 2026-06-29 verification). source_official set to false.

Israel (90 days), Malaysia (30 days), and Singapore (30 days) listed by Wikipedia as visa-free - no corresponding bilateral agreement or special official source found on any official Solomon Islands immigration site. These countries are not on the prior-approval list so receive the standard on-arrival visitor permit. The specific 30-day/90-day limits mentioned by Wikipedia cannot be confirmed or denied from official sources. No entry added without an official source.
No 'conditional access based on third-country credentials' (e.g., entry because holder has a valid US/Schengen/Australian visa/residence) was found on any official Solomon Islands immigration source.
No OCI cardholder exemption or special diaspora status provision found on any official source.
The Citizenship by Investment Bill status beyond mid-2025 could not be confirmed from an accessible official page (solomons.gov.sb redirected with HTTP 307).
No official RBI, digital nomad, or skilled-worker fast-track programs found on official government sites.
No e-visa or ETA system is currently operational per official immigration site. Online payment described as 'coming soon'.

EU agreement: France and Netherlands partial exclusion (European territory only) confirmed from EUR-Lex. UK exclusion confirmed. Ireland exclusion confirmed. Danish territories status not explicitly addressed in available summary.
Visa fees for Yacht Person, Journalist, Maritime Transit, and several other categories not explicitly stated on official page.
Processing time guarantees are not published by Solomon Islands Immigration - the site explicitly states 'there is no guarantee timeframe'.

Visitor Visa Tourist page states on-arrival grant is 30 days; the general Immigration Act visitor permit allows up to 3 months. The precise relationship between these two tracks (whether all visitors get the Act-based 90-day permit or the specific tourist visa 30-day grant) is ambiguous from official sources alone.

outbound_official.note: No official Solomon Islands government outbound visa-list page located. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs and External Trade site (mfaet.gov.sb) returned HTTP 500 errors throughout the search session and does not appear to publish an enumerated outbound visa-free list for Solomon Islands citizens. The Immigration Division site (immigration.gov.sb) covers only inbound visa requirements for visitors to Solomon Islands. The Solomon Islands High Commission (sihc.org.nz) page at /index.php/visa-passports/ returned HTTP 404. The solomons.gov.sb portal's travel/visa page redirects without accessible content. No other *.gov.sb or official diplomatic domain was found to host an authoritative outbound visa-free list. Rely on destination-side data.
